📰 Thank you Christianity Today, @ct_mag , for highlighting CityTeam Portland’s compassionate, solutions-driven response to Oregon’s persisting drug crisis. The author interviews CityTeam Portland’s executive director, Lance Orton, about his lived experience overcoming addiction and homelessness and how he’s dedicated his life to removing others from the grips of addiction. 

We are so proud of our Portland team for their outstanding work, Christ-centered leadership, and dedication to transforming their community—one individual at a time. 💙🙏 #CityTeamPO #FaithInAction #Restoration 

Read the full article in our bio or at:
christianitytoday.com/2024/11/oregon-drug-decriminalization-portland-ministries-recovery/

We're thrilled to partner with @benito_link as they use a generous grant from @getspectrum to bring computer support and digital literacy resources to the seniors we serve in Hollister!

Access to technology helps older adults stay connected with loved ones, access healthcare, manage finances, and navigate everyday life with greater confidence. Together, we're helping bridge the digital divide and empowering seniors with the skills they need to thrive.

Thank you to BenitoLink and Spectrum for investing in our community and making a meaningful difference in the lives of local seniors. 💙💻

#MarthasKitchen #BenitoLink #Spectrum #DigitalInclusion #SeniorServices #HollisterCA #CommunityPartnership #BridgingTheDigitalDivide

Zero Waste Innovations Grant Spotlight: @MarthasKitchenSJ

Martha’s Kitchen provides nutritious meals in schools, senior centers, shelters, and more, by mobilizing volunteers and rescued food ensuring everyone gets access. Their project will expand its edible food recovery infrastructure, which will increase donated meals, reduce hunger, while preventing landfilled food waste.

Learn more about their organization and how you can get involved at Marthas-Kitchen.org.

This program is funded by the City of San José Zero Waste Innovations Grant (ZWIG), which supports creative, community-driven solutions that reduce solid waste, while promoting sustainability and a circular economy. Learn more about the #ZWIG program and our grantees at SJEnvironment.org/ZWIGrants.

A heartfelt thank you to the Intero Foundation for supporting senior meal services in San Benito County! 💙

Between July 2025 and March 2026, Martha's Kitchen delivered 20,000 meals to 150 homebound seniors, helping ensure our neighbors could age with dignity and access nutritious food from the comfort of their homes.

During that same period, we served 290 seniors and provided 10,000 congregate meals, creating opportunities for connection, community, and nourishment.

Your support is making a meaningful difference in the lives of local seniors every day. Thank you for helping us ensure that no senior has to worry about where their next meal will come from. #MarthasKitchen #SanBenitoCounty #SeniorNutrition #CommunitySupport #InteroFoundation

As families continue to struggle with the high cost of living, thousands of local students lack the basic school supplies they need to start the school year ready to learn.

According to the 2025 Silicon Valley Pain Index:

- More than 2,200 San José students are experiencing homelessness, nearly triple the number reported in 2020.
- Santa Clara County has the nation's highest rate of unsheltered, unaccompanied homeless youth.
- More than 26,500 children in the county live below the federal poverty level.

Each year, Sacred Heart Community Service provides backpacks and school supplies to more than 3,500 students, with more than 900 new families joining the program last year alone.

You can also be part of supporting Pack a Back 2026 by volunteering, sharing this post, donating and getting involved! 

More information in the link in our bio! 

#sacredheartcs #communityunited

The #SanJose villa is open for summer. Couple up with water conservation by making a few small habits part of your daily routine. Every drop you save helps keep our water clean and ready for everyone. Your future self will thank you. #loveisland 🏝️
 
For more summer water savings tips, visit SJEnvironment.org/WaterEfficiency

🚲 Thaco Thursday Tours #3: "Tour de Hoover!" with our friends at @Western Wheelers Bicycle Club

This month's TTT takes us through Stanford's most iconic "Hoover-centric" landmarks — a school, a park, a historic home, a memorial plaza, a medical campus, and the crown jewel: Hoover Tower itself! 🗼
This shorter-than-usual route gives us ample time to soak in the lofty Hoover Tower vistas, and enjoy delicious tacos and more at La Cazuela, timed close to the lunch hour! 🌮
📍 Group size is capped at 20 due to Hoover Tower visitor requirements, so register now before all of the spaces are gone:
👉 https://bit.ly/svbe-ttt-july16 (or go to the svbe.org website, volunteer/calendar and register from the link shown on July 16)

📋 Important Info:
• Hoover Tower entry fee is $8/person (no cash — card only)
• Bring a lock to secure bikes at the racks near the front entrance
• Wear or pack walkable shoes — hard floors up top!
• We'll spend 40+ minutes at the tower soaking in the unique 360° views from this 285 ft, 85-year-old icon

🕘 Ride Details:
• All TTT rides start from SVBE: arrive 9:45am, roll out 10am
• 20 miles, mellow 340 ft of gain
• Hoover Tower stop at mile 10.2
• • Lunch at La Cazuela, timed near the lunch hour
Snack Stop at @patagoniapaloalto, mile 12
• Social pace, quiet residential roads & paths — no one gets dropped!

✅ Reminders:
• Check that your tires & brakes are trustworthy
• Helmets REQUIRED
• Nice weather expected — mid-to-high 70s near the bay! Bring a water bottle and wear light/bright colors to stay visible 🌞

See you Thursday! 🚴‍♀️🚴‍♂️

(Hoover Tower photo attribution: King of Hearts / Wikimedia Commons / CC-BY-SA-3.0)

Nominations Are Open for the 2026 Silicon Valley Kindness Awards!

Who inspires you? Who leads with courage, compassion, and action?

Sunday Friends is seeking nominations for the 2026 Silicon Valley Kindness Awards, recognizing the extraordinary individuals, organizations, volunteers, advocates, innovators, and business leaders who are transforming our communities through kindness in action.

At Sunday Friends, we believe Revolutionary Kindness is more than a gesture—it is action that challenges injustice, creates opportunity, and strengthens our communities. We're looking for the Kindness Warriors whose commitment to others is creating lasting change across Silicon Valley.

2026 Award Categories

Business Award – Honoring companies that strengthen communities through partnership and opportunity.

Education Award – Recognizing organizations that expand access to education and opportunity.

Philanthropy Award – Honoring foundations and individuals whose generosity expands opportunity, strengthens dignity, and drives lasting change through trust, partnership, and sustained investment in community.

Volunteer Award – Recognizing individuals whose service strengthens families and communities.

Civic Award – Celebrating public leaders advancing innovative solutions and economic opportunity.

Lifetime Achievement Award – Honoring extraordinary leadership and a lifetime of community impact.

Do you know someone who embodies extraordinary compassion, leadership, and service? Nominate them today and help us celebrate the people building stronger, healthier, and more connected communities.

Awards Celebration: September 18, 2026
Adobe World Headquarters, San Jose

🔗 Submit your nomination today: >>Check Link in Bio<<

Nominations close August 1, 2026.

Please help us spread the word by sharing this post with your friends, colleagues, and community networks.

Because extraordinary people build extraordinary communities.

Happy 4th of July from Sunnyvale Community Services! ❤️

True freedom and independence begin with having a safe place to sleep and food on the table. Every day, we strive to embody this service by standing alongside our most vulnerable neighbors and ensuring that no one goes hungry or without a home.

Wishing our wonderful community a warm, safe, and happy holiday! ✨

Through storytelling and hands-on service, 'Seeds of Compassion', our family volunteering program, helps instill the value of compassion in action from an early age.

On the first Saturday of June, families came together to  organize and clean our food pantry, and prepare resources for neighbors facing hardship. Alongside these meaningful volunteer activities, participants learned about the challenges many families in our community face and how even small acts of kindness can make a lasting difference.

Thank you to all the wonderful families who joined us. Your compassion, generosity, and willingness to serve are helping build a stronger, more caring community 💙
